mapping.a_element: empirical spectral distribution of complex random matrices with i.i.d. entries (mean 0, variance 1/n)
mapping.b_element: correlation functions of ensembles of asymmetric real matrices (e.g., real Ginibre ensemble)

mapping.a_element: convergence to uniform distribution over the unit disk (circular law) via potential theory and extreme singular values
mapping.b_element: scaling limits (bulk and edge) of correlation functions via Pfaffian of an antisymmetric matrix kernel

mapping.a_element: finite fourth moment assumption on matrix elements
mapping.b_element: asymptotic analysis of matrix size (no explicit moment condition mentioned, but scaling limits imply regularity)

brief_justification: While both papers study large random matrices, Paper A uses potential theory and extreme singular value analysis to prove spectral convergence to the circular law, whereas Paper B derives correlation functions via Pfaffian kernels and scaling limits. The core machinery (potential theory vs. Pfaffian determinants) and objectives (spectral distribution vs. correlation functions) differ fundamentally.
